title: Add one more animated mascot character

requirements.text: Add one additional moving cartoon-style mascot element (original, non-copyrighted) with a lightweight animation to make the site feel more fun, and render it on the Mathematics page without interfering with content interaction (decorative only).

quotes: add one more character animation to make it even more fun.

acceptanceCriteria: A new mascot image is displayed on the Mathematics page (frontend/src/pages/MathematicsPage.tsx) as a decorative, animated element.
The mascot does not block clicks/taps on page content (uses pointer-events-none) and is marked decorative for assistive tech (empty alt and/or aria-hidden=true).
The animation is lightweight and consistent with existing motion styles (e.g., float/bounce-gentle/wiggle) and is disabled when the user prefers reduced motion (e.g., via motion-reduce:animate-none).
The mascot is responsive and does not overlap important UI on common breakpoints (hidden on small screens or positioned safely).

requirements.text: Add the new mascot image as a local static asset under frontend/public/assets/generated and reference it from the new mascot element using the same asset conventions as existing mascots.

quotes: add one more character animation to make it even more fun.

acceptanceCriteria: The new mascot image file exists in frontend/public/assets/generated with the exact filename specified in this build request.
The new mascot is loaded via a static /assets/generated/... path (no backend/image proxy).
No existing mascot assets (e.g., mascot-robotcat.dim_768x768.png, mascot-girl.dim_768x768.png) are removed or broken.

constraints: Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui (read-only).
Do not introduce copyrighted characters (e.g., Dora, Doraemon); use an original mascot design.
Keep the backend architecture unchanged (single Motoko actor); no backend changes are required for this request.
nonGoals: Do not redesign site navigation or add new learning modules.
Do not change the existing gender theme behavior beyond what is necessary to ensure the new mascot respects the current styling and reduced-motion preferences.
